logging(3) Library Functions Manual logging(3)

NAME

logging - Logging System


- 📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

SYNOPSIS

Files


file colorize.c
Log message colorization for terminal output. file format.c
Log format parser implementation. file json.c
📝 JSON structured logging output using yyjson file log.c
📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output file mmap.c
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging implementation. file named.c
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file colorize.h
Log message colorization for terminal output. file json.h
📝 JSON structured logging output file log.h
📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control file mmap.h
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging with crash safety. file named.h
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file types.h
Log level types and constants.

Data Structures


struct log_buffer_entry_t
A single buffered log entry.

Default log level for debug builds (DEBUG and above). #define MAX_LOG_SIZE (3 * 1024 * 1024)
Maximum log file size in bytes (3MB) before rotation. #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_SIZE (64 * 1024)
Maximum size of terminal output buffer (64KB). #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_ENTRIES 256
Maximum number of buffered log entries. #define LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 4096
Maximum size of a single log message (including formatting). #define LOG_MMAP_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 1024
Maximum size of a log message in mmap mode. #define LOG_HEADER_BUFFER_SIZE 512
Maximum size of a log header (timestamp, level, file:line:func). #define LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE 32
Maximum size of a timestamp string. #define log_plain(...)
Plain logging - writes to both log file and stderr without timestamps or log levels. #define log_plain_stderr(...)
Plain logging to stderr with newline. #define log_plain_stdout(...)
Plain logging to stdout with newline. #define log_plain_stderr_nonewline(...)
Plain logging to stderr without newline - for interactive prompts. #define log_file(...)
File-only logging - writes to log file only, no stderr output. #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)
Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)
Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). #define log_once(log_level, fmt, ...)
Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). #define LOG_CLIENT_IMPL(client, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends DEBUG log message to client. #define log_info_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends INFO log message to client. #define log_warn_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends WARN log message to client. #define log_error_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends ERROR log message to client. #define log_fatal_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends FATAL log message to client. #define LOG_SERVER_IMPL(sockfd, crypto_ctx, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends DEBUG log message to server. #define log_info_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends INFO log message to server. #define log_warn_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends WARN log message to server. #define log_error_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends ERROR log message to server. #define log_fatal_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends FATAL log message to server.

Logging Macros

Note

Compile-time log level stripping: In release builds, log_dev() and log_debug() are compiled out completely (no runtime overhead). Override with LOG_COMPILE_LEVEL.


#define log_dev(...)
Log a DEV message (most verbose, development only). #define log_debug(...)
Log a DEBUG message. #define log_info(...)
Log an INFO message. #define log_warn(...)
Log a WARN message. #define log_error(...)
Log an ERROR message. #define log_fatal(...)
Log a FATAL message.

Detailed Description

📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

This header provides a comprehensive logging system with:

  • Multiple log levels (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
  • File and terminal output with automatic color coding
  • Terminal capability detection (16-color, 256-color, truecolor)
  • Rate-limited logging macros for high-frequency logging
  • Thread-safe logging operations
  • Automatic log file rotation when size limit is reached

Note

In debug builds, log macros include file/line/function information. In release builds, this information is omitted.

This module formats named objects in log messages by scanning for hex addresses and replacing them with friendly descriptions from the named object registry when available.

For example:

  • Input: "Locked 0x7f1234567890 successfully"
  • Output: "Locked mutex: recv_mutex.2 (0x7f1234567890) successfully"

When an address is not registered, it's left unchanged.

#define
  log_every( log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \


  uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \


  uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \


  if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \


    if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \


      log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \


    }                                                                                                                \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). Logs at most once per
    specified time interval. Useful for threads that have an FPS and functions
    they call to prevent spammy logs.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


interval_us Minimum microseconds between log messages


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Each call site maintains its own static atomic timer, so
  different call sites can log independently. Thread-safe via atomic
  compare-exchange.



Uses platform_get_monotonic_time_us() for cross-platform
    time.




Definition at line 671 of file log/log.h.


671 #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)                                                                    \
672   do {                                                                                                                 \
673     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \
674     uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \
675     uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \
676     if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \
677       if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \
678         log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \
679       }                                                                                                                \
680     }                                                                                                                  \
681   } while (0)

#define
  log_nth( log_level, n, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \


  uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \


  uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \


  atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \


  if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). Logs a
    message every nth time the code is executed. Useful for logging periodic
    events in tight loops without spamming the log. For example, log_nth(INFO,
    1000, "Processed items") logs every 1000 calls.




Each call site maintains its own static counter, so different call
    sites can log independently at different frequencies.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


n Log every nth call (1 = every call, 2 = every 2nd call, etc.)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic fetch_add.

740   do {                                                                                                                 \
741     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \
742     uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \
743     uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \
744     atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \
745     if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \
746       log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \
747     }                                                                                                                  \
748   } while (0)










#define
  log_once( log_level, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_once_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                            \


  uint64_t _log_once_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_once_counter);                         \


  if (_log_once_count == 0) {                                                                                        \


    atomic_store_u64(&_log_once_counter, 1);                                              \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). Logs a message
    exactly once, no matter how many times the code is executed. Each call site
    maintains its own static counter, so different call sites can log
    independently.




Useful for one-time initialization messages, warnings, or debug
    output that should only appear once per session.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic operations.

enum log_color_t


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Color enum for logging - indexes into color arrays. These values
    directly index into level_colors arrays. Order matches DEV, DEBUG, WARN,
    INFO, ERROR, FATAL, GREY, RESET.


Enumerator
  





  
LOG_COLOR_DEV
    

  
Blue - DEV messages

  
LOG_COLOR_DEBUG
    

  
Cyan - DEBUG messages

  
LOG_COLOR_INFO
    

  
Green - INFO messages

  
LOG_COLOR_WARN
    

  
Yellow - WARN messages

  
LOG_COLOR_ERROR
    

  
Red - ERROR messages

  
LOG_COLOR_FATAL
    

  
Magenta - FATAL messages

  
LOG_COLOR_GREY
    

  
Grey - for neutral messages or labels

  
LOG_COLOR_RESET
    

  
Reset to default

Function
  Documentation




const
  char * colorize_named_string (const char * name_str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Colorize a "type/name" string for display.


Parameters


name_str String in "type/name" or
  "type/name.id" format


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes:
  type(yellow)/name(blue)


Used by memory report and other displays to colorize named
    objects. Applies colors:


    
  
  • Type: yellow (LOG_COLOR_WARN)
    • 
  
  • Name: blue (LOG_COLOR_DEV)
    • 
  
  • Separator: uncolored
    • 



Example: "thread/splash_anim.0" displays with type in
    yellow, name in blue




Note


Returns pointer to rotating static buffer (4 buffers)



Safe to use in printf-style calls: "%s"




Colorize a "type/name" string for display.




Parameters


name_str String in "type/name
  (0xaddress)" format (e.g., "thread/splash_anim.0
  (0x7f123456)")


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes: type(yellow)/name(blue)
  (id(grey))


Used by memory report to colorize named object display. Returns a
    pointer to a static rotating buffer (4 buffers, valid until next call).




Example: "thread/splash_anim.0 (0x7f123456)" displays
    with:


    
  
  • "thread" in yellow
    • 
  
  • "splash_anim.0" in blue
    • 
  
  • "0x7f123456" in grey
    • 



Definition at line 796 of file log/named.c.


796                                                         {
797 #define COLORIZE_BUFFERS 4
798 #define COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE 512
799   static char buffers[COLORIZE_BUFFERS][COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE];
800   static int buffer_idx = 0;
801 
802   if (!name_str || name_str[0] == '\0') {
803     return name_str;
804   }
805 
806   char *current_buf = buffers[buffer_idx];
807   buffer_idx = (buffer_idx + 1) % COLORIZE_BUFFERS;
808 
809   /* Find the slash separator (type/name) */
810   const char *slash = strchr(name_str, '/');
811   if (!slash) {
812     /* No slash found - just return the string as-is */
813     return name_str;
814   }
815 
816   /* Find opening paren for address */
817   const char *paren = strchr(name_str, '(');
818 
819   /* Extract type (before slash) */
820   size_t type_len = slash - name_str;
821   char type_buf[256];
822   strncpy(type_buf, name_str, type_len < sizeof(type_buf) - 1 ? type_len : sizeof(type_buf) - 1);
823   type_buf[type_len] = '\0';
824 
825   /* Extract name (between slash and paren, or end of string) */
826   const char *name_start = slash + 1;
827   size_t name_len;
828   if (paren && paren > slash) {
829     /* Skip spaces before paren */
830     const char *name_end = paren - 1;
831     while (name_end > name_start && *name_end == ' ') {
832       name_end--;
833     }
834     name_len = name_end - name_start + 1;
835   } else {
836     name_len = strlen(name_start);
837   }
838 
839   char name_buf[256];
840   strncpy(name_buf, name_start, name_len < sizeof(name_buf) - 1 ? name_len : sizeof(name_buf) - 1);
841   name_buf[name_len] = '\0';
842 
843   /* Apply colors */
844   const char *type_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, type_buf);
845   const char *name_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, name_buf);
846 
847   /* Format: type(yellow)/name(blue) and add address part if present */
848   if (paren) {
849     /* Extract address (0x...) from parentheses */
850     const char *addr_start = paren + 1;
851     const char *addr_end = strchr(addr_start, ')');
852     if (addr_end) {
853       size_t addr_len = addr_end - addr_start;
854       char addr_buf[128];
855       strncpy(addr_buf, addr_start, addr_len < sizeof(addr_buf) - 1 ? addr_len : sizeof(addr_buf) - 1);
856       addr_buf[addr_len] = '\0';
857 
858       const char *addr_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, addr_buf);
859       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s (%s)", type_colored, name_colored, addr_colored);
860     } else {
861       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s %s", type_colored, name_colored, paren);
862     }
863   } else {
864     safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s", type_colored, name_colored);
865   }
866 
867   return current_buf;
868 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFERS
869 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE
870 }






References colored_string(), COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE,
    COLORIZE_BUFFERS, LOG_COLOR_DEV, LOG_COLOR_GREY,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, and safe_snprintf().






char
  * format_message (const char * format, va_list args)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Format a message using va_list.


Parameters


format Format string


args Variable arguments list


Returns


Formatted message string (must be freed by caller)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }






References args, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    safe_vsnprintf().

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get current time as formatted string.


Parameters


time_buf Output buffer for formatted time


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null
  terminator)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Destroy the logging system and close log file.


Definition at line 622 of file log/log.c.


622                        {
623   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
624   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
625     log_mmap_destroy();
626   }
627 
628   // Cleanup grep filter
629   grep_destroy();
630 
631   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
632    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
633    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
634    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
635   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
636 
637   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
638   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
639   if (g_log.format) {
640     log_template_free(g_log.format);
641     g_log.format = NULL;
642   }
643   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
644     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
645     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
646   }
647   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
648 
649   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
650   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
651   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
652     platform_close(old_file);
653   }
654   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
655 
656   // Destroy rotation mutex
657   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
658 }

asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap (const char *
  log_path)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap-based logging. When enabled, log messages
    bypass the mutex and use atomic operations to write directly to a
    memory-mapped log file as human-readable text.




Benefits:


    
  
  • No mutex contention between logging threads
    • 
  
  • Crash-safe: text is written directly to mmap'd file, readable after
    crash
    • 
  
  • Fast path uses atomic fetch_add, no locks
    • 
  
  • ERROR/FATAL messages sync immediately for visibility
    • 
  
  • Simple: log file IS the mmap file (no separate binary format)
    • 



Parameters


log_path Path to the log file (will be
  memory-mapped)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Call log_init() first, then
  log_enable_mmap() to upgrade to lock-free


Definition at line 1753 of file log/log.c.


1753                                                         {
1754   return log_enable_mmap_sized(log_path, 0); /* Use default size */
1755 }






References log_enable_mmap_sized().






asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap_sized (const char *
  log_path, size_t max_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap logging with custom file size.


Parameters


log_path Path to the log file


max_size Maximum file size in bytes (0 = default 4MB)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1757 of file log/log.c.


1757                                                                                {
1758   if (!log_path) {
1759     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "log_path is required");
1760   }
1761 
1762   // Initialize mmap logging - text is written directly to the mmap'd file
1763   asciichat_error_t result = log_mmap_init_simple(log_path, max_size);
1764   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1765     return result;
1766   }
1767 
1768   log_info("Lock-free mmap logging enabled: %s", log_path);
1769   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1770 }

    log_info, log_mmap_init_simple(), and SET_ERRNO.

void
  * log_get_template (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get the current log format template (opaque pointer).


Returns


Opaque pointer to the current log format template (may be
  NULL if not set) Cast to log_template_t* after including
  log/format.h


Returns the compiled log format template used by the logging
    system. This is useful for code that needs to format log entries using the
    same template as the rest of the logging system (e.g., platform code).




Note


The returned pointer is valid only for the lifetime of
  the logging system



It's safe to call before log_init() (will return NULL)




Return type is void* (opaque) to avoid circular dependency with
    format.h

Returns

    system. This is used by platform code (e.g., backtrace formatting) to format

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Initialize the logging system.


Parameters


filename Log file path (or NULL for no file
  logging)


level Minimum log level to output


force_stderr If true, route ALL logs to stderr (for client mode to keep
  stdout clean)


use_mmap If true, use fully lock-free mmap logging (recommended). If mmap
  fails, uses stderr only (no mutex fallback).


Note


When use_mmap=true, the entire logging path is lock-free:

    
  
  • File output uses atomic operations on mmap'd memory
    • 
  
  • Terminal output uses atomic fprintf/fwrite to fd
    • 
  
  • No mutex is ever acquired in the hot path
    • 





Definition at line 532 of file log/log.c.


532                                                                                          {
533   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
534   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
535   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
536   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
537 
538   // Set basic config using atomic stores
539   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
540   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
541 
542   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
543   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
544   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
545   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
546     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
547   }
548 
549   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
550   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
551   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
552     platform_close(old_file);
553     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
554   }
555 
556   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
557   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
558   if (env_level_str) {
559     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
560   } else {
561     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
562   }
563 
564   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
565   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
566 
567   if (filename) {
568     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
569 
570     if (use_mmap) {
571       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
572       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
573       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
574         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
575       } else {
576         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
577         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
578           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
579         }
580         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
581         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
582       }
583     } else {
584       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
585       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
586       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
587       if (fd < 0) {
588         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
589           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
590         }
591         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
592       }
593     }
594   } else {
595     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
596     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
597   }
598 
599   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
600   log_set_format(NULL, false);
601 
602   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
603   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
604     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
605   }
606 
607   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
608 
609   // Reset terminal detection if needed
610   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
611     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
612   }
613 
614   // Detect terminal capabilities
615   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
616 
617   // NOTE: Color initialization happens separately via log_set_color_scheme()
618   // after options are parsed. Logging works without colors until then.
619   // NOTE: Grep filter initialization happens in main.c after options_init() completes.
620 }

1664     return;
1665   }
1666

1668   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
1669     return;
1670   }
1671 
1672   if (g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1673     return;
1674   }
1675 
1676   /* Get active color scheme - this ensures color system is initialized */
1677   const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
1678   if (!scheme) {
1679     /* Don't mark as initialized if we can't get a color scheme - return NULL instead */
1680     return;
1681   }
1682 
1683   /* Acquire mutex for compilation (mutex is now initialized by colorscheme_init) */
1684   mutex_lock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);

1686   /* Zero the structure on first use to avoid freeing garbage pointers */
1687   /* (static = {0} produces garbage in this build for unknown reasons) */

1691     first_compile = false;
1692   }
1693 
1694   /* Detect terminal background */
1695   terminal_background_t background = detect_terminal_background();
1696   /* Determine color mode for compilation */
1697   terminal_color_mode_t mode;
1698   if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
1699     mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
1700   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1701     mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
1702   } else {
1703     mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
1704   }
1705   /* Compile the color scheme to ANSI codes */
1706   asciichat_error_t result = colorscheme_compile_scheme(scheme, mode, background, &g_log.compiled_colors);
1707   g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized = true;
1708   mutex_unlock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1709 
1710   /* Log outside of mutex lock to avoid recursive lock deadlock */
1711   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1712     log_debug("Failed to compile color scheme: %d", result);
1713   }

bool log_lock_terminal (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Lock terminal output for exclusive access by the calling thread.
    Call this before interactive prompts (like password entry, yes/no questions)
    to ensure only the calling thread can output to the terminal. Other threads'
    log messages will be buffered and flushed when the terminal is unlocked.




While locked:


    
  
  • The locking thread can use log_plain() to write to terminal
    • 
  
  • Other threads' log messages go to log file and are buffered
    • 
  
  • Buffered messages are flushed to terminal on unlock
    • 



Must be paired with log_unlock_terminal().




Returns


The previous terminal lock state (for nested calls)

785   bool expected = atomic_load_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked);
786   while (!atomic_cas_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked, &expected, true)) {
787     // Retry if CAS failed
788   }
789   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread, (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self());
790   return expected;
791 }

1035 
1036     va_list args;

1041

1045     }
1046 
1047     // Validate UTF-8 in formatted message

1049

1051

1053     if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled) && !atomic_load_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked)) {
1054       char time_buf[LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE];
1055       uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1056       get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);
1057 
1058       // Choose output stream using unified routing logic
1059       int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1060       FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1061       // Check if colors should be used
1062       // Priority 1: If --color was explicitly passed, force colors
1063       bool use_colors = true; // Default: enable colors
1064       if (g_color_flag_passed && !g_color_flag_value) {
1065         use_colors = false; // --color=false explicitly disables colors
1066       }
1067       // Priority 2: If --color NOT explicitly passed, enable colors by default
1068 
1069       char header_buffer[512];
1070       int header_len = format_log_header(header_buffer, sizeof(header_buffer), level, time_buf, file, line, func,
1071                                          use_colors, time_ns);
1072 
1073       if (header_len >= 0 && header_len < (int)sizeof(header_buffer)) {
1074         // Platform-specific log hook (e.g., for WASM browser console)
1075         platform_log_hook(level, msg_buffer);
1076 
1077         if (use_colors) {
1078           const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(msg_buffer);
1079           const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
1080           if (colors) {
1081             safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s%s%s\n", header_buffer, colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET], colorized_msg,
1082                          colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET]);
1083           } else {
1084             safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s\n", header_buffer, colorized_msg);
1085           }
1086         } else {
1087           safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s\n", header_buffer, msg_buffer);
1088         }
1089         (void)fflush(output_stream);
1090       }
1091     }
1092     return;
1093   }
1094   /* =========================================================================
1095    * FILE I/O PATH: Lock-free using atomic write() syscalls
1096    * ========================================================================= */
1097   char time_buf[LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE];
1098   uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1099   get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);
1100   // Format message for file output
1101   char log_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1102   va_list args;
1103   va_start(args, fmt);
1104   int header_len = format_log_header(log_buffer, sizeof(log_buffer), level, time_buf, file, line, func, false, time_ns);
1105   if (header_len < 0 || header_len >= (int)sizeof(log_buffer)) {
1106     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format log header");
1107     va_end(args);
1108     return;
1109   }
1110   int msg_len = header_len;
1111   int formatted_len = safe_vsnprintf(log_buffer + header_len, sizeof(log_buffer) - (size_t)header_len, fmt, args);
1112   if (formatted_len < 0) {
1113     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format log message");
1114     va_end(args);
1115     return;
1116   }
1117 
1118   msg_len += formatted_len;
1119   // Truncate at whole line boundaries to avoid UTF-8 issues
1120   msg_len = truncate_at_whole_line(log_buffer, msg_len, sizeof(log_buffer));
1121   // Add newline if there's room and message doesn't already end with one
1122   if (msg_len > 0 && msg_len < (int)sizeof(log_buffer) - 1) {
1123     if (log_buffer[msg_len - 1] != '\n') {
1124       log_buffer[msg_len++] = '\n';
1125       log_buffer[msg_len] = '\0';
1126     }
1127   }
1128   va_end(args);
1129   // Validate UTF-8 in formatted message
1130   validate_log_message_utf8(log_buffer, "leveled log message");
1131 
1132   // Extract the user message part (without header) for JSON logging
1133   const char *user_message = log_buffer + header_len;
1134 
1135   // For JSON output: strip automatic trailing newline (keep explicit ones)
1136   char json_message_buf[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1137   const char *json_message = user_message;
1138   size_t user_msg_len = strlen(user_message);
1139 
1140   // If message ends with newline and it was added automatically (not in original message)
1141   if (user_msg_len > 0 && user_message[user_msg_len - 1] == '\n') {
1142     // The newline was added automatically if the message part didn't end with one
1143     // before we added it on line 1067. We can check this by looking at msg_len - 1
1144     // If msg_len >= header_len + 2, then msg_len - 2 would be the char before the newline
1145     int msg_content_len = msg_len - header_len;
1146     if (msg_content_len > 1 && log_buffer[msg_len - 2] != '\n') {
1147       // Newline was added automatically - strip it for JSON

1156   // Check if JSON format is enabled
1157   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);

1159 
1160   // If JSON format is enabled, output ONLY JSON (skip text output)
1161   if (json_format_enabled) {
1162     // Output JSON to the JSON file descriptor
1163     log_json_write(json_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1164     // Also output JSON to console using unified routing logic, respecting quiet flag
1165     if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1166       int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1167       log_json_write(console_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1168     }
1169   } else {
1170     // Text format: output to file and terminal
1171     // No heap allocation - use log_buffer directly
1172     // Write to file (atomic write syscall) - use original buffer (with ANSI codes)
1173     int file_fd = (int)atomic_load_u64(&g_log.file);
1174     if (file_fd >= 0 && file_fd != STDERR_FILENO) {
1175       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1176     }
1177 
1178     // Write to terminal (atomic state checks)
1179     va_list args_terminal;
1180     va_start(args_terminal, fmt);
1181     write_to_terminal_atomic(level, time_buf, file, line, func, fmt, args_terminal, time_ns);
1182     va_end(args_terminal);
1183   }
1184 }

int
  log_named_format_message (const char * message, char * output, size_t
  output_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format hex addresses in a message as named object
  descriptions.


Parameters


message Input message (may contain hex addresses
  like 0x123456)


output Output buffer for formatted message


output_size Size of output buffer (must be > 0)


Returns


Length of output string (excluding null terminator) if
  any formatting was applied, -1 if no formatting was applied or on error


Scans the input message for hex addresses in the format
    0x[0-9a-fA-F]+. For each address found, checks the named object registry. If
    registered, replaces the address with "type: name (0xaddress)"
    format. If not registered, leaves the original hex address unchanged.




The output buffer is always null-terminated.




Note


The formatting is idempotent - applying it twice gives
  the same result



This function is efficient for messages with few hex addresses
    (common case)




Works correctly with 32-bit and 64-bit address formats




Definition at line 467 of file log/named.c.


467                                                                                     {
468   if (!message || !output || output_size == 0) {
469     return -1;
470   }
471 
472   size_t out_pos = 0;
473   const char *p = message;
474   bool any_transformed = false;
475 
476   while (*p && out_pos < output_size - 1) {
477     /* Look for "0x" prefix indicating a hex address */
478     if (*p == '0' && *(p + 1) == 'x' && isxdigit(*(p + 2))) {
479       const char *hex_start = p;
480       if (try_format_hex_address(message, hex_start, output, output_size, &out_pos, &p)) {
481         any_transformed = true;
482         continue;
483       }
484 
485       /* Not registered or formatting failed - copy original hex address */
486       const char *hex_end = hex_start + 2; /* Start after "0x" */
487       while (*hex_end && isxdigit(*hex_end)) {
488         hex_end++;
489       }
490       size_t hex_len = hex_end - hex_start;
491       if (hex_len < output_size - out_pos) {
492         memcpy(output + out_pos, hex_start, hex_len);
493         out_pos += hex_len;
494         p = hex_end;
495       } else {
496         /* Buffer overflow - truncate and stop */
497         break;
498       }
499     } else if (isdigit(*p)) {
500       /* Look for decimal integers that might be FDs or packet types */
501       const char *int_start = p;
502       int fd_value = 0;
503       int digit_count = 0;
504 
505       /* Parse decimal digits */
506       while (*p && isdigit(*p) && digit_count < 6) { /* FDs rarely exceed 999999 */
507         fd_value = fd_value * 10 + (*p - '0');
508         p++;
509         digit_count++;
510       }
511 
512       /* Skip if this integer is part of already-formatted output like "(fd=20)" or "(type/name (...))" */
513       bool is_already_formatted = false;
514       if (int_start - message >= 5) {
515         /* Check for "(fd=", "(pkt_type=", "(socket=", "(sockfd=", or "(type/" patterns to prevent re-formatting.
516          * Named objects are formatted as: type/name (0xaddress) or type/name (key=value)
517          * We need to detect if we're already inside a formatted output.
518          */
519         const char *check = int_start - 1;
520         while (check > message && isspace(*check))
521           check--;
522 
523         // Only access *check if pointer is within bounds
524         if (check >= message && *check == '=') {
525           /* Found "=", now check what prefix it has for patterns like "(fd=20)" or "(socket=20)" or "(pkt_type=123)" */
526           const char *eq_pos = check;
527           check--;
528           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
529             check--;
530           }
531           check++;
532 
533           size_t prefix_len = eq_pos - check;
534           if ((prefix_len == 2 && strncmp(check, "fd", 2) == 0) ||
535               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "socket", 6) == 0) ||
536               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "sockfd", 6) == 0) ||
537               (prefix_len == 8 && strncmp(check, "pkt_type", 8) == 0)) {
538             is_already_formatted = true;
539           }
540         } else if (check >= message && *check == '/') {
541           /* Found "/", now check if this is a type/name pattern.
542            * Format is "(type/name (...)" where type is word characters (thread, mutex, socket, etc.)
543            * Must verify "/" is preceded by word characters AND "(" to ensure it's a formatted type.
544            */
545           const char *slash_pos = check;
546           check--;
547           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
548             check--;
549           }
550           check++;
551 
552           /* Check that the "/" was preceded by word characters (indicating a type name) */
553           size_t type_len = slash_pos - check;
554           if (type_len > 0 && (isalpha(*check) || *check == '_')) {
555             /* Verify this is a registered type/name pattern by checking for "(" before the type
556              * AND checking if the type actually exists in the registry */
557             const char *type_start = check;
558             const char *before_type = type_start - 1;
559             while (before_type > message && isspace(*before_type)) {
560               before_type--;
561             }
562 
563             /* Only mark as formatted if:
564              * 1. Preceded by "(" - ensures pattern looks like "(type/name ...)"
565              * 2. Type exists in registry - prevents false positives on random text */
566             if (before_type >= message && *before_type == '(' && is_type_in_registry(type_start, type_len)) {
567               is_already_formatted = true;
568             }
569           }
570         }
571       }
572 
573       /* Check if this is a registered packet type */
574       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_packet_type_prefix(message, int_start)) {
575         const char *name = named_get_packet_type(fd_value);
576         if (name) {
577           const char *prefix_start = find_packet_type_prefix_start(message, int_start);
578           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
579           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
580 
581           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
582             out_pos -= prefix_len;
583           }
584 
585           if (write_formatted_packet_type(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
586             any_transformed = true;
587             continue;
588           }
589 
590           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
591         }
592       }
593 
594 
595       /* Check if this is a registered file descriptor */
596       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_fd_prefix(message, int_start)) {
597         const char *name = named_get_fd(fd_value);
598         if (name) {
599           const char *prefix_start = find_fd_prefix_start(message, int_start);
600           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
601           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
602 
603           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
604             out_pos -= prefix_len;
605           }
606 
607           if (write_formatted_fd(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
608             any_transformed = true;
609             continue;
610           }
611 
612           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
613         }
614       }
615 
616       /* Check if this integer has a generic type prefix (socket, client, connection, etc.)
617        * DISABLED: This feature has a backtracking bug that corrupts output when applied after
618        * other formatting operations. Re-enable only after fixing the backtracking logic to
619        * properly track correspondence between input and output positions.
620        * See commit b86fed2a8 which introduced the bug.
621        */
622       if (false && !is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0) {
623         const char *type_name = NULL;
624         size_t type_len = 0;
625         const char *prefix_start = find_generic_type_prefix(message, int_start, &type_name, &type_len);
626 
627         if (type_name && prefix_start != int_start) {
628           /* Skip if already formatted with a name (e.g., "socket/listener.0" already has a /) */
629           bool skip_format = false;
630           /* Check if this looks like it's already been formatted by looking for / in the pattern */
631           const char *check_ptr = prefix_start;
632           while (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr && *check_ptr != '/') {
633             check_ptr++;
634           }
635           if (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr == '/') {
636             /* Already formatted (contains /), skip */
637             skip_format = true;
638           }
639 
640           if (!skip_format) {
641             /* We found a type prefix like "socket", "client", etc.
642              * Try to look up a registered name for this type+id */
643 
644             /* Normalize type name aliases: "sockfd" → "socket", "descriptor" → "fd" */
645             const char *lookup_type = type_name;
646             size_t lookup_len = type_len;
647             if (type_len == 6 && strncmp(type_name, "sockfd", 6) == 0) {
648               lookup_type = "socket";
649               lookup_len = 6;
650             } else if (type_len == 10 && strncmp(type_name, "descriptor", 10) == 0) {
651               lookup_type = "fd";
652               lookup_len = 2;
653             }
654 
655             const char *name = named_get_by_type_and_id(lookup_type, lookup_len, fd_value);
656 
657             char temp_output[512];
658             char type_str[32];
659             char id_buffer[64];
660 
661             /* Safely copy type name */
662             if (type_len < sizeof(type_str)) {
663               memcpy(type_str, type_name, type_len);
664               type_str[type_len] = '\0';
665 
666               int id_written = snprintf(id_buffer, sizeof(id_buffer), "%d", fd_value);
667               if (id_written > 0 && id_written < (int)sizeof(id_buffer)) {
668                 int temp_written = 0;
669                 if (name) {
670                   /* Format with name: type/name (type=value) */
671                   temp_written =
672                       snprintf(temp_output, sizeof(temp_output), "%s/%s (%s=%s)", type_str, name, type_str, id_buffer);
673 
674                   if (temp_written > 0 && (size_t)temp_written < sizeof(temp_output)) {
675                     /* Backtrack to remove the prefix we already copied */
676                     size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
677                     if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
678                       out_pos -= prefix_len;
679                     }
680 
681                     int copy_len = temp_written;
682                     if (out_pos + copy_len < output_size - 1) {
683                       memcpy(output + out_pos, temp_output, copy_len);
684                       out_pos += copy_len;
685                       any_transformed = true;
686                       continue;
687                     }
688                   }
689                 }
690               }
691             }
692           }
693         }
694       }
695 
696       /* Not a registered FD or formatting failed - copy original number */
697       if (!copy_unformatted_decimal(int_start, p, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
698         break;
699       }
700     } else {
701       /* Check if this is the start of "type=DIGIT" pattern */
702       if (*p == 't' && p + 4 < message + strlen(message) && is_type_equals_pattern(p)) {
703         if (!type_equals_inside_parens(p, message)) {
704           const char *digit_end = p + 5;
705           int pkt_value = parse_decimal_digits(digit_end, &digit_end);
706 
707           if (pkt_value > 0) {
708             const char *name = named_get_packet_type(pkt_value);
709             if (name && write_formatted_type_equals(pkt_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
710               p = digit_end;
711               any_transformed = true;
712               continue;
713             }
714           }
715         }
716       }
717 
718       /* Regular character - copy it */
719       copy_char_to_output(output, &out_pos, *p++);
720     }
721   }
722 
723   /* Null terminate */
724   if (out_pos < output_size) {
725     output[out_pos] = '\0';
726   } else {
727     output[output_size - 1] = '\0';
728   }
729 
730   return any_transformed ? (int)out_pos : -1;
731 }

const
  char * log_named_format_or_original (const char * message)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format named objects in a message (thread-local buffer).


Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string if any formatting was applied,
  original message if no formatting was applied


Convenience wrapper that handles buffer management internally
    using a thread-local buffer. The returned pointer is valid only until the
    next call to this function from the same thread.




Safe to use directly in log macros:




log_info("State: %s", log_named_format_or_original(msg));



Note


Maximum formatted message length is 4096 bytes



If format buffer overflows, returns original message




Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string (thread-local buffer, valid only
  until next call)


Convenience wrapper that uses a thread-local buffer internally.
    Applies formatting iteratively until the message stops changing (fixpoint).
    Returns the original message if no formatting was applied.


Definition at line 742 of file log/named.c.


742                                                               {
743   if (!message) {
744     return message;
745   }
746 
747   static _Thread_local char format_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
748   static _Thread_local char work_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
749 
750   /* Apply formatting iteratively until the message stabilizes (no more changes) */
751   const char *current = message;
752   size_t max_iterations = 3; /* Reduce iterations to prevent recursion (prevent transforming already-transformed output) */
753 
754   for (size_t iter = 0; iter < max_iterations; iter++) {
755     int result = log_named_format_message(current, format_buffer, sizeof(format_buffer));
756 
757     if (result <= 0) {
758       /* No transformation was made, we've reached a fixpoint */
759       return current == message ? message : format_buffer;
760     }
761 
762     /* Check if the message changed */
763     if (strcmp(current, format_buffer) == 0) {
764       /* No actual change, return the current version */
765       return format_buffer;
766     }
767 
768     /* Message changed, prepare for next iteration */
769     if (current == message) {
770       /* First iteration, use format_buffer as input for next iteration */
771       current = format_buffer;
772     } else {
773       /* Subsequent iterations, swap buffers */
774       strcpy(work_buffer, format_buffer);
775       current = work_buffer;
776     }
777   }
778 
779   /* Max iterations reached, return last formatted version */
780   return format_buffer;
781 }

    NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE.

1250       }

1252       write_to_log_file_atomic("\n", 1, NULL);
1253     }
1254   }
1255 
1256   // Terminal output (atomic state checks)
1257   if (!atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1258     return;
1259   }

1275     log_json_write(console_fd, LOG_INFO, time_ns, __FILE__, __LINE__, "log_plain_msg", log_buffer);
1276   } else {
1277     // Choose output stream using unified routing logic (LOG_INFO level)
1278     int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1279     FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1280 
1281     // Apply colorization for TTY output
1282     if (terminal_should_color_output(fd)) {
1283       const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(log_buffer);
1284       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", colorized_msg);
1285     } else {
1286       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", log_buffer);
1287     }
1288     (void)fflush(output_stream);
1289   }
1290 }

void
  log_plain_stdout_msg (const char * fmt, ...)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Plain logging to stdout with newline.


Parameters


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Converts a plain text log line (from log file) into a colored version
    matching the format used for terminal output. Applies colors to:


    
  
  • Timestamp and level based on log level
    • 
  
  • Thread ID in grey
    • 
  
  • File path in cyan
    • 
  
  • Line number in magenta
    • 
  
  • Function name in orange/DEV color
    • 
  
  • Message body colorized appropriately
    • 



Expected plain format (debug mode): [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL]
    [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE




Parameters


plain_line Plain text log line (from log file)


colored_buf Output buffer for colored version (must be large enough)


buf_size Size of colored_buf


Returns


Length of colored string, 0 on error or invalid
  format


Note


Uses static buffer internally, result is reused across
  calls


Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID]
    FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes
    matching the colored format.


Definition at line 1859 of file log/log.c.


1859                                                                                            {
1860   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1861     return 0;
1862   }
1863 
1864   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1865 
1866   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1867   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1868   const char *p = plain_line;
1869 
1870   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1871   if (*p != '[') {
1872     return 0; // Invalid format
1873   }
1874   p++;
1875   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1876 
1877   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1878   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1879   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1880     p++;
1881   }
1882   if (*p != ']') {
1883     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1884   }
1885 
1886   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1887   char timestamp[64];
1888   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1889     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1890   }
1891   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1892   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1893   p++; // Skip ]
1894 
1895   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1896   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1897     p++;
1898   }
1899 
1900   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1901   if (*p != '[') {
1902     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1903   }
1904   p++;
1905   const char *level_start = p;
1906   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1907     p++;
1908   }
1909   if (*p != ']') {
1910     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1911   }
1912   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1913   char level_str[16];
1914   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1915     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1916   }
1917   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1918   strncpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1919   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1920 
1921   // Determine log level for color selection
1922   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1923   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1924     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1925   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1926     level = LOG_INFO;
1927   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1928     level = LOG_WARN;
1929   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1930     level = LOG_ERROR;
1931   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1932     level = LOG_FATAL;
1933   }
1934 
1935   p++; // Skip ]
1936 
1937   // Skip whitespace after level
1938   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1939     p++;
1940   }
1941 
1942   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1943   uint64_t tid = 0;
1944   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1945     p += 5;
1946     char *tid_end = NULL;
1947     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1948     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1949       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1950       // Try to find the closing bracket
1951       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1952         p++;
1953       }
1954       if (*p == ']') {
1955         p++;
1956       }
1957     } else {
1958       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1959     }
1960     // Skip whitespace after tid
1961     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1962       p++;
1963     }
1964   }
1965   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1966 
1967   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1968   const char *file_start = p;
1969   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1970     p++;
1971   }
1972   if (*p != ':') {
1973     return 0; // Malformed
1974   }
1975   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1976   char file_path[256];
1977   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1978     return 0;
1979   }
1980   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1981   strncpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
1982   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
1983   p++; // Skip :
1984 
1985   // Extract line number (digits only)
1986   int line_num = 0;
1987   const char *line_start = p;
1988   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
1989     p++;
1990   }
1991   if (p == line_start) {
1992     return 0; // No line number
1993   }
1994   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
1995 
1996   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
1997   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
1998     p++;
1999   }
2000 
2001   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
2002   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
2003     p += 3; // Skip "in "
2004   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
2005     // Allow tab after "in"
2006     p += 2; // Skip "in"
2007     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
2008       p++;
2009     }
2010   } else {
2011     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
2012     return 0;
2013   }
2014 
2015   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
2016   const char *func_start = p;
2017   while (*p && *p != '(') {
2018     p++;
2019   }
2020   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
2021     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
2022   }
2023   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
2024 
2025   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
2026   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
2027     func_len--;
2028   }
2029 
2030   char func_name[256];
2031   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
2032     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
2033   }
2034   if (func_len > 0) {
2035     /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
2036     strncpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
2037   }
2038   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
2039 
2040   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
2041   if (*p == '(') {
2042     p++;
2043     while (*p && *p != ')') {
2044       p++;
2045     }
2046     if (*p == ')') {
2047       p++;
2048     }
2049   }
2050 
2051   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
2052   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
2053     p++;
2054   }
2055 
2056   // Remaining is the message
2057   const char *message = p;
2058 
2059   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
2060   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
2061   if (!colors) {
2062     // No colors available, return plain text
2063     static bool warned_once = false;
2064     if (!warned_once) {
2065       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
2066       warned_once = true;
2067     }
2068     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2069     if (len >= buf_size) {
2070       return 0;
2071     }
2072     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2073     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2074     return len;
2075   }
2076 
2077   // Build colored output
2078   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2079   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2080   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2081   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2082   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2083   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2084 
2085   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2086                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2087                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2088                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2089 
2090   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2091     return 0;
2092   }
2093 
2094   // Colorize the message part
2095   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2096   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2097                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2098                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2099                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2100 
2101   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2102     return 0;
2103   }
2104 
2105   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2106   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2107   return (size_t)len;
2108 }

void
  log_redetect_terminal_capabilities (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Re-detect terminal capabilities after logging is initialized.
    Useful when terminal capabilities change or need to be refreshed.


Definition at line 1594 of file log/log.c.


1594                                               {
1595   // Guard against recursion
1596   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1597     return;
1598   }
1599 
1600   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1601   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1602   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1603   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
1604     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = true;
1605     g_log.terminal_caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1606     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1607     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1608 
1609     // Now log the capabilities AFTER colors are set, so this log uses the correct colors
1610     log_debug("Terminal capabilities: color_level=%d, capabilities=0x%x, utf8=%s, fps=%d",
1611               g_log.terminal_caps.color_level, g_log.terminal_caps.capabilities,
1612               g_log.terminal_caps.utf8_support ? "yes" : "no", g_log.terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1613 
1614     // Now that we've detected once with reliable results, keep these colors consistent for all future logs
1615   }
1616   // Once initialized with reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1617 }

743   if (g_log.format) {
744     log_template_free(g_log.format);
745     g_log.format = NULL;
746   }
747   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
748     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
749     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
750   }
751 
752   /* Use default format if NULL or empty string */
753   const char *format_to_use = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0') ? format_str : OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT;
754   bool is_custom = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0');
755 
756   /* Parse the format string (always parse, never skip) */
757   log_template_t *parsed_format = log_template_parse(format_to_use, false);
758 
759   if (!parsed_format) {
760     log_error("Failed to parse log format: %s", format_to_use);
761     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid log format string");
762   }
763 
764   /* If console_only is true, we also need the default format for file output */
765   if (console_only && is_custom) {
766     log_template_t *default_format = log_template_parse(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT, false);
767 
768     if (!default_format) {
769       log_template_free(parsed_format);
770       log_error("Failed to parse default log format");
771       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to parse default format");
772     }
773     g_log.format = default_format;
774     g_log.format_console_only = parsed_format;
775   } else {
776     g_log.format = parsed_format;
777     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
778   }
779 
780   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.has_custom_format, is_custom);
781   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
782 }

697                                            {
698   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
699   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
700 
701   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
702   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
703   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
704   if (enabled) {

  log_shutdown_begin (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Begin shutdown phase - disable console logging but keep file
    logging. Call this before logging shutdown messages. Disables console output
    but keeps file logging so messages are recorded for debugging.




Useful when you want final messages (like "no servers
    found") to go to the log file only, not to stdout where it might
    interfere with output.

format Compiled format (from log_template_parse)


buf Output buffer


buf_size Output buffer size


level Log level


timestamp Pre-formatted timestamp string


file Source file name (or NULL)


line Source line number (or 0)


func Function name (or NULL)


tid Thread ID


message Log message text


use_colors If true, apply ANSI color codes


time_nanoseconds Raw wall-clock time in nanoseconds (for microseconds and
  nanoseconds)


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null terminator),
  or -1 on error


Renders the compiled format using provided log entry values.
    Evaluates each specifier and writes results to output buffer.




Example:




log_template_t *fmt = log_template_parse("[%time(%H:%M:%S)] [%level] %microseconds %message", false);
char output[512];
uint64_t now = time_get_realtime_ns();
int len = log_template_apply(fmt, output, sizeof(output),


                           LOG_INFO, "14:30:45", "test.c", 42, "main",


                           1234, "Test message", false, now);



Definition at line 484 of file log/format.c.


486                                                                                         {
487   /* Check all critical pointers - format could be freed by another thread (TOCTOU race) */
488   if (!format || !buf || buf_size == 0) {
489     return -1;
490   }
491 
492   int total_written = 0;
493   char *p = buf;
494   size_t remaining = buf_size - 1; /* Reserve space for null terminator */
495 
496   (void)timestamp; /* May be used in future; for now, LOG_FORMAT_TIME uses custom formatting */
497 
498   /* Safety check: verify format->specs is valid before dereferencing
499    * This protects against use-after-free if format is freed by another thread (TOCTOU race).
500    * Between our initial NULL check and here, the main thread may have freed this memory. */
501   if (!format || !format->specs || format->spec_count == 0) {
502     return -1;
503   }
504 
505   for (size_t i = 0; i < format->spec_count; i++) {
506     /* Re-check specs pointer in loop to catch use-after-free during iteration */
507     if (!format->specs || i >= format->spec_count) {
508       break; /* Format was freed, stop processing */
509     }
510     const log_format_spec_t *spec = &format->specs[i];
511 
512     /* Additional safety check: if spec is null or invalid, stop processing */
513     if (!spec) {
514       break;
515     }
516 
517     int written = 0;
518 
519     switch (spec->type) {
520     case LOG_FORMAT_LITERAL:
521       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", spec->literal ? spec->literal : "");
522       break;
523 
524     case LOG_FORMAT_TIME:
525       /* Format time using custom time formatter */
526       if (spec->literal) {
527         written = time_format_now(spec->literal, p, remaining + 1);
528         if (written <= 0) {
529           log_debug("time_format_now failed for format: %s", spec->literal);
530           written = 0;
531         }
532       }
533       break;
534 
535     case LOG_FORMAT_LEVEL:
536       /* Log level as string (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL) */
537       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", get_level_string(level));
538       break;
539 
540     case LOG_FORMAT_LEVEL_ALIGNED:
541       /* Log level padded to exactly 5 characters (prevents truncation to [DEBU]/[ERRO]) */
542       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", get_level_string_padded(level));
543       break;
544 
545     case LOG_FORMAT_FILE:
546       if (file) {
547         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", file);
548       }
549       break;
550 
551     case LOG_FORMAT_FILE_RELATIVE:
552       if (file) {
553         const char *rel_file = extract_project_relative_path(file);
554         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", rel_file);
555       }
556       break;
557 
558     case LOG_FORMAT_LINE:
559       if (line > 0) {
560         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%d", line);
561       }
562       break;
563 
564     case LOG_FORMAT_FUNC:
565       if (func) {
566         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", func);
567       }
568       break;
569 
570     case LOG_FORMAT_TID:
571       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%llu", (unsigned long long)tid);
572       break;
573 
574     case LOG_FORMAT_TNAME: {
575       asciichat_thread_t thread = (asciichat_thread_t)tid;
576       uintptr_t key = asciichat_thread_to_key(thread);
577       const char *tname = named_get(key);
578       if (tname) {
579         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", tname);
580       } else {
581         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%llu", (unsigned long long)tid);
582       }
583       break;
584     }
585 
586     case LOG_FORMAT_MICROSECONDS: {
587       /* Extract microseconds from nanoseconds (ns_value % 1_000_000_000 / 1000) */
588       long nanoseconds = (long)(time_nanoseconds % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
589       long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
590       if (microseconds < 0)
591         microseconds = 0;
592       if (microseconds > 999999)
593         microseconds = 999999;
594       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%06ld", microseconds);
595       break;
596     }
597 
598     case LOG_FORMAT_NANOSECONDS: {
599       /* Extract nanoseconds component (ns_value % 1_000_000_000) */
600       long nanoseconds = (long)(time_nanoseconds % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
601       if (nanoseconds < 0)
602         nanoseconds = 0;
603       if (nanoseconds > 999999999)
604         nanoseconds = 999999999;
605       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%09ld", nanoseconds);
606       break;
607     }
608 
609     case LOG_FORMAT_STRFTIME_CODE: {
610       /* strftime format code (like %H, %M, %S, %A, %B, etc.)
611        * Let strftime handle all the parsing and validation */
612       if (spec->literal && spec->literal_len < 64) {
613         /* Construct format string with % prefix on stack */
614         char format_str[66]; /* 1 (%) + 64 (literal) + 1 (null) */
615         format_str[0] = '%';
616         memcpy(format_str + 1, spec->literal, spec->literal_len);
617         format_str[spec->literal_len + 1] = '\0';
618         written = time_format_now(format_str, p, remaining + 1);
619         if (written <= 0) {
620           log_debug("time_format_now failed for format code: %s", format_str);
621           written = 0;
622         }
623       }
624       break;
625     }
626 
627     case LOG_FORMAT_MESSAGE:
628       if (message) {
629         /* Format hex addresses as named object descriptions (type/name format) */
630         const char *formatted_msg = log_named_format_or_original(message);
631         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", formatted_msg);
632       }
633       break;
634 
635     case LOG_FORMAT_COLORED_MESSAGE: {
636       /* Apply colorize_log_message() for number/unit/hex highlighting, then format named objects */
637       if (message) {
638         const char *formatted_msg = log_named_format_or_original(message);
639         const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(formatted_msg);
640         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", colorized_msg);
641       }
642       break;
643     }
644 
645     case LOG_FORMAT_COLORLOG_LEVEL:
646       /* Color code for the log level (placeholder for future color support) */
647       (void)use_colors; /* Suppress unused parameter warning */
648       written = 0;
649       break;
650 
651     case LOG_FORMAT_COLOR: {
652       /* Parse and apply %color(LEVEL, content)
653        * literal stores "LEVEL,content" where LEVEL is the color level name (or "*" for current level)
654        * and content is a format string to render and colorize */
655       if (!spec->literal || spec->literal_len == 0) {
656         written = 0;
657         break;
658       }
659 
660       /* Find the comma separating LEVEL and content */
661       const char *comma_pos = strchr(spec->literal, ',');
662       if (!comma_pos) {
663         /* Invalid format - no comma found */
664         log_debug("log_template_apply: %%color format missing comma in: %s", spec->literal);
665         written = 0;
666         break;
667       }
668 
669       /* Extract level name (before comma) */
670       size_t level_len = comma_pos - spec->literal;
671       char level_name[32];
672       if (level_len >= sizeof(level_name)) {
673         /* Level name too long */
674         written = 0;
675         break;
676       }
677       memcpy(level_name, spec->literal, level_len);
678       level_name[level_len] = '\0';
679 
680       /* Parse level name to log_color_t (pass current level for "*" support) */
681       log_color_t color = parse_color_level(level_name, level);
682 
683       /* Extract content (after comma), skip leading whitespace */
684       const char *content_start = comma_pos + 1;
685       while (*content_start == ' ' || *content_start == '\t') {
686         content_start++;
687       }
688 
689       /* Render content recursively */
690       char content_buf[512];
691       int content_len = render_format_content(content_start, content_buf, sizeof(content_buf), level, timestamp, file,
692                                               line, func, tid, message, use_colors, time_nanoseconds);
693 
694       if (content_len < 0 || content_len >= (int)sizeof(content_buf)) {
695         written = 0;
696         break;
697       }
698 
699       /* Apply color to rendered content using colored_string */
700       const char *colored_content = colored_string(color, content_buf);
701 
702       /* Copy colored content to output buffer */
703       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", colored_content);
704       break;
705     }
706 
707     case LOG_FORMAT_NEWLINE:
708       /* Platform-aware newline */
709 #ifdef _WIN32
710       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "\r\n");
711 #else
712       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "\n");
713 #endif
714       break;
715 
716     default:
717       break;
718     }
719 
720     if (written < 0) {
721       /* snprintf error */
722       return -1;
723     }
724 
725     if ((size_t)written > remaining) {
726       /* Buffer overflow prevention */
727       log_debug("log_template_apply: buffer overflow prevented");
728       return -1;
729     }
730 
731     p += written;
732     remaining -= written;
733     total_written += written;
734   }
735 
736   *p = '\0';
737   return total_written;
738 }

log_template_t * log_template_parse (const char *
  format_str, bool console_only)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/format.h>


Parse a format string into compiled format structure.


Parameters


format_str Format string (e.g.,
  "[%time(%H:%M:%S)] [%level_aligned] %message")


console_only If true, format applies only to console (not file)


Returns


Compiled format, or NULL on parse error


Parses format string and compiles it into efficient specifier
    array. Format string must be valid UTF-8. Returns NULL on:


    
  
  • Invalid UTF-8 in format string
    • 
  
  • Invalid format specifiers
    • 
  
  • Malformed time(format) syntax
    • 
  
  • Memory allocation failure
    • 



Note


Errors are logged with details about what failed

void
  log_terminal_msg (log_level_t level, const char * file, int line, const
  char * func, const char * fmt, ...)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Log a message to terminal only (no file output).


Parameters


level Log level (LOG_DEV, LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO,
  LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR, LOG_FATAL)


file Source file name (or NULL to omit)


line Source line number (or 0 to omit)


func Function name (or NULL to omit)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Logs to terminal only, skipping file/mmap output. WARN/ERROR/FATAL
    go to stderr, other levels go to stdout (unless force_stderr is
  enabled).

void
  log_truncate_if_large (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Manually truncate large log files. Checks if the log file exceeds
    MAX_LOG_SIZE and truncates it if necessary.


Definition at line 804 of file log/log.c.


804                                  {
805   // Log rotation is inherently racy without locks - best-effort only
806   // For reliable rotation, use mmap mode or external logrotate
807   int file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
808   if (file >= 0 && file != STDERR_FILENO && strlen(g_log.filename) > 0) {
809     struct stat st;
810     if (fstat(file, &st) == 0 && st.st_size > MAX_LOG_SIZE) {
811       atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, (size_t)st.st_size);
812     }
813   }
814 }
